texts.full_text: n this Study, we explored whether a single prior exposure to the light/dark (L/D) exploration test could similarly affect the anxiolytic response to chlordiazepoxide (CDP). Ethological scoring methods were employed to assess anxiety-Like behaviors, including line crossings, time spent in the light compartment, and stretched attend postures (SAPs). One group of adult male Swiss-Webster mice was given a single undrugged exposure to the L/D test 24 h prior to drug testing, while another group was completely naïve to the

texts.full_text: heir left nostril. The area was not bleeding and there were no known medical conditions that would cause this condition, such as blood transfusions or nosebleeds due to poor circulation (known medically as hemophilia A) prior history of nasal packing.) Results of an examination of the right nasal cavity were unremarkable. Oral cavity examination revealed a well aligned complete set of teeth for his age, normal soft palate, hard palate, and tongue.
